questions.question:
${S}_{{1}}: {H}_{3} {C}-{C} \equiv {CH}$ has restricted rotation.
${S}_{{2}}$ : Cyclic compounds have also restricted rotation.
${S}_{3}: {CH}_{3}-{CH}_{3}$ will show geometrical isomerism due to restricted rotation.
${S}_{{4}}: {H}_{3} {C}-{C} \equiv {N}$ : has restricted rotation so it shows geometrical isomerism.
questions.question_raw:
{tex}{S}_{{1}}: {H}_{3} {C}-{C} \equiv {CH}{/tex} has restricted rotation.
{tex}{S}_{{2}}{/tex} : Cyclic compounds have also restricted rotation.
{tex}{S}_{3}: {CH}_{3}-{CH}_{3}{/tex} will show geometrical isomerism due to restricted rotation.
{tex}{S}_{{4}}: {H}_{3} {C}-{C} \equiv {N}{/tex} : has restricted rotation so it shows geometrical isomerism.
questions.explanation:
The solution is correct because:
1. Statement S1: The compound {tex}H_3C-C \equiv CH{/tex} has a triple bond, which restricts rotation around that bond. Therefore, this statement is true.
2. Statement S2: Cyclic compounds typically have restricted rotation due to their ring structure, which prevents free rotation around the bonds. Thus, this statement is also true.
3. Statement S3: The compound {tex}CH_3-CH_3{/tex} (ethane) does not have restricted rotation because it is a single bond, allowing free rotation. Therefore, this statement is false.
4. Statement S4: The compound {tex}H_3C-C \equiv N{/tex} has a triple bond, which restricts rotation. However, geometrical isomerism requires the presence of different groups on either side of the bond, which is not the case here. Thus, this statement is false. The correct evaluation of the statements leads to the conclusion that the answer is T T F F.

2. Ketones: These have the general structure RC(=O)R'. For C5H10O, we can have: - 3 different ketones by varying the carbon chain and position of the carbonyl group. Adding the possible aldehydes and ketones gives a total of 4 (aldehydes) + 3 (ketones) = 7 distinct aliphatic carbonyl compounds. Thus, the solution correctly identifies that there are 7 possible compounds.

options.text: Both statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is incorrect but statement II is correct
Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect
Both statement I and Statement II are correct

questions.explanation: Methyl orange is a weak acid. So, statement- 1 is correct. In acidic medium, it exists in quinonoid form which is red in colour and in alkaline medium it exists in benzenoid form which is yellow in colour. questions.question: Choose the halogen which is most reactive towards ${S}_{{N}} 1$ reaction in the given compounds (A, B, C, & D)
questions.question_raw: Choose the halogen which is most reactive towards {tex}{S}_{{N}} 1{/tex} reaction in the given compounds (A, B, C, & D)
questions.explanation:
Organic compounds are more reactive towards SN1, if the carbocation is stable compared to others. Reactivity in reactions depends upon the stability of the carbocation intermediate.


  1. {tex}\rightarrow \operatorname{Br}({a}){/tex}, Because formed intermediate carbocation get stable by conjugation with phenyl ring

  2. {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} I(a), Because formed intermediate carbocation become more stable by conjugation

  3. {tex}\rightarrow \operatorname{Br}({b}){/tex}, Because we can't remove {tex}\operatorname{Br}({a}){/tex} from bridge head carbon (Bredt's rule)

  4. {tex}\rightarrow \operatorname{Br}({a}){/tex}, because formed intermediate {tex}3^{\circ}{/tex} carbocation is more stable (stability of carbocation {tex}3^{\circ} \gt 2^{\circ} \gt 1^{\circ}{/tex})

questions.question: Cobalt chloride when dissolved in water forms pink colored complex $\underline{X}$ which has octahedral geometry. This solution on treating with conc. HCl forms deep blue complex, $\underline{{Y}}$ which has a $\underline{{Z}}$ geometry. $X, Y$ and $Z$, respectively, are
questions.question_raw: Cobalt chloride when dissolved in water forms pink colored complex {tex}\underline{X}{/tex} which has octahedral geometry. This solution on treating with conc. HCl forms deep blue complex, {tex}\underline{{Y}}{/tex} which has a {tex}\underline{{Z}}{/tex} geometry. {tex}X, Y{/tex} and {tex}Z{/tex}, respectively, are
questions.explanation:
{tex}{X}=\left[{Co}\left({H}_{2} {O}\right)_{6}\right]^{2+},({Y})=\left[{CoCl}_{4}\right]^{2-},({Z})={/tex} Tetrahedral
The {tex}\left[{Co}\left({H}_{2} {O}\right)_{6}\right]^{2+}{/tex} when treated with excess HCl (conc.) turns deep blue in color. This is because the complex absorbs an orange light (low-frequency light). This is due to the lower splitting of d-orbitals of {tex}{Co}^{2+}{/tex} ions. The tetrahedral complexes have a lower splitting than the octahedral complexes and absorb light of lower frequency. Hence, the geometry of the complex {tex}({Y}){/tex} is tetrahedral.

Match items of column I and II:
 Column I
 (Mixture of compounds)
 Column II
 (Separation Technique)
 A.
 ${H}_{2} {O} / {CH}_{2} {Cl}_{2}$
 i.
 Crystallization
 B.
 ii.
 Differential solvent extraction
 C.
 Kerosene/Naphthalene
 iii.
 Column chromatography
 D.
 ${C}_{6} {H}_{12} {O}_{6} / {NaCl}$

 Fractional Distillation
questions.question_raw:
Match items of column I and II:

  Column I
(Mixture of compounds)		   Column II
(Separation Technique)
A. {tex}{H}_{2} {O} / {CH}_{2} {Cl}_{2}{/tex} i. Crystallization
B. ii. Differential solvent extraction
C. Kerosene/Naphthalene iii. Column chromatography
D. {tex}{C}_{6} {H}_{12} {O}_{6} / {NaCl}{/tex} iv. Fractional Distillation
questions.explanation:
• The solvent extraction method is used to separate liquids with the difference in densities. The density of {tex}{CH}_{2} {Cl}_{2}{/tex} is greater than the density of water.
  • The p-nitrophenol has intermolecular H-bonding. Hence, it is separated by column chromatography.
  • Kerosene and naphthalene are separated by fractional distillation due to the difference in the boiling points.
  • NaCl is an ionic compound and {tex}{C}_{6} {H}_{12} {O}_{6}{/tex} is a covalent compound. {tex}{C}_{6} {H}_{12} {O}_{6}{/tex} is soluble in an organic solvent. Hence, they can be separated by crystallization.

questions.question: Identify the correct order of reactivity for the following pairs towards the respective mechanism
questions.question_raw: Identify the correct order of reactivity for the following pairs towards the respective mechanism
questions.explanation:
1. Steric hindrance (crowding) is the basis of {tex}{S}_{{N}} 2{/tex} reaction.
    Rate of {tex}{S}_{{N}} 2 \propto \frac{1}{\text { Steric crowding at 'C' bearing leaving group }}{/tex}.
    As steric hinderance (crowding) increases, rate of {tex}{S}_{{N}} 2{/tex} reaction decreases.
    Note: The order of reactivity towards {tex}{S}_{{N}} 2{/tex} reaction for alkyl halides is:
    Methyl > Primary halides ({tex}1^{\circ}{/tex}) > ({tex}2^{\circ}{/tex}) > Tertiary halides ({tex}3^{\circ}{/tex})
  2. Reactivity of {tex}{S}_{{N}} 1{/tex} reaction depends on the stability of the carbocation formed. Higher the stability of carbocation, higher will be its reactivity.
    Stability of carbocation follows the order,
    Tertiary > Secondary > Primary
    (B) {tex}{S}_{{N}} 1 \rightarrow{/tex} reactivity {tex}\times{/tex} Stability of Carbocation formed

    The rate of electrophilic substitution depends on the nature of the substituent already present in the benzene ring.
  3. If the substituent is {tex}{o} / {p}{/tex} directing (activating groups) then the rate of substitution increases. If it is meta directing (deactivating groups) then the rate of substitution decreases. Electron-withdrawing groups destabilize the carbocation intermediate of electrophilic aromatic substitution. All activating substituents increase the rate of electrophilic aromatic substitution and are ortho-para directors.
    Electrophilic Substitution reaction{tex}\text { rate } \propto \frac{1}{\text { EWG }}{/tex}
    The presence of nitro group ({tex}-{NO}_{2}{/tex}) at ortho/para positions increase the reactivity of haloarenes towards nucleophilic substitution reaction.
    This is because nitro group ({tex}-{NO}_{2}{/tex}) at o/p positions withdraw the electrons from the benzene ring which facilitates the attack of the nucleophile. The negative charge in the carbanion formed at o/p positions with respects to halogen atom is stabilized by the presence of nitro groups ({tex}-{NO}_{2}{/tex}) and resonance respectively.
  4. Nueleophilic substitution: rate {tex}\times{/tex} no. of EWG attached at benzene.

Correct statements for the given reaction are:

  2. The completion of above reaction is very slow
  3. 'A' shows tautomerism
  4. The bond lengths of {tex}{C}-{C}{/tex} in compound B are found to be same

questions.explanation: The compound (B) is aromatic in nature because it follows Huckel rule of aromaticity. It contains {tex}6 \pi {e}^{-}{/tex} and it is cyclic, planar and has complete conjugation. It also shows tautomerism to form keto-enol compound. The reaction is very fast because it is an example of acid-base reaction. Due to the presence of conjugation, the bond length of C-C bond is same throughout the molecules.
